title: Capturing Structural Locality in Non-parametric Language Models

structured.summary: This paper proposes a method to enhance non-parametric language models by incorporating structural locality information through learned, locality-conditioned linear adjustments to retrieval distances. Evaluated on Wikipedia text and Java source code, the approach improves perplexity and token prediction accuracy by leveraging hierarchical locality features without retraining the base language model.

strengths.point: Novel incorporation of structural locality into kNN-LMs via learned distance modifications
strengths.evidence: The paper introduces a learned linear function to adjust retrieval distances based on multiple hierarchical locality levels, improving neighbor relevance.

strengths.point: Demonstrated improvements across two diverse domains with distinct locality structures
strengths.evidence: Experiments on WikiText-103 and Java GitHub datasets show consistent perplexity and accuracy gains when incorporating locality features.

strengths.point: Lightweight approach requiring no retraining of the base parametric LM
strengths.evidence: Locality parameters are trained separately on a validation set, adding minimal overhead and preserving the original LM.

strengths.point: Interpretability through learned parameters that emphasize more local contexts
strengths.evidence: Qualitative examples illustrate how locality-aware adjustments boost probabilities of relevant neighbors and suppress less relevant ones.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient comparison to prior hierarchical and cluster-aware retrieval methods
weaknesses.evidence: The paper lacks thorough literature review and empirical baselines involving hierarchical kNN, cluster-based reranking, or metadata-aware retrieval.

weaknesses.point: Limited exploration and justification of the linear distance modification function
weaknesses.evidence: No ablations or theoretical motivation are provided for choosing a linear form over nonlinear alternatives.

weaknesses.point: Binary locality indicators restrict expressiveness without exploring graded or hierarchical measures
weaknesses.evidence: Locality functions are defined as binary indicators with no experiments on continuous or soft similarity metrics.

weaknesses.point: Training locality parameters solely on validation set raises overfitting and robustness concerns
weaknesses.evidence: No details on cross-validation, regularization, or robustness checks are provided.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation metrics focus on perplexity and top-1 accuracy, omitting retrieval-specific metrics
weaknesses.evidence: Metrics such as recall@k or mean reciprocal rank are not reported, limiting understanding of retrieval quality.

weaknesses.point: Lack of analysis on computational overhead, scalability, and datastore construction details
weaknesses.evidence: No quantitative measurements of memory, latency, or indexing methods are given, nor discussion on scaling to large or streaming datastores.

weaknesses.point: Limited generalizability demonstrated beyond two domains with well-defined locality
weaknesses.evidence: No experiments or discussion on applicability to other domains with less structured or noisy locality.

weaknesses.point: No error analysis or study of failure cases where locality adjustments may harm performance
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not analyze instances where locality fails to improve or degrades language modeling.

questions.question: How does the choice of linear versus nonlinear distance modification functions affect performance?
questions.motivation: Understanding this could reveal whether more expressive models capture complex locality effects better.

questions.question: Can the approach generalize to domains with noisy, missing, or less hierarchical locality information?
questions.motivation: Real-world datasets often have imperfect metadata; robustness to such conditions is critical for practical deployment.

questions.question: What is the computational cost and scalability of incorporating locality-aware distance adjustments in large or streaming datastores?
questions.motivation: Assessing overhead is essential to evaluate feasibility in production systems.

questions.question: How does the quality or architecture of the base parametric LM influence the gains from locality-aware retrieval?
questions.motivation: This could guide when and how to best apply the method depending on existing LM capabilities.

critique_points.point_001: Insufficient comparison to prior hierarchical and cluster-aware retrieval methods. The paper lacks thorough literature review and empirical baselines involving hierarchical kNN, cluster-based reranking, or metadata-aware retrieval
critique_points.point_002: Limited exploration and justification of the linear distance modification function. No ablations or theoretical motivation are provided for choosing a linear form over nonlinear alternatives
critique_points.point_003: Binary locality indicators restrict expressiveness without exploring graded or hierarchical measures. Locality functions are defined as binary indicators with no experiments on continuous or soft similarity metrics
critique_points.point_004: Training locality parameters solely on validation set raises overfitting and robustness concerns. No details on cross-validation, regularization, or robustness checks are provided
critique_points.point_005: Evaluation metrics focus on perplexity and top-1 accuracy, omitting retrieval-specific metrics. Metrics such as recall@k or mean reciprocal rank are not reported, limiting understanding of retrieval quality
critique_points.point_006: Lack of analysis on computational overhead, scalability, and datastore construction details. No quantitative measurements of memory, latency, or indexing methods are given, nor discussion on scaling to large or streaming datastores
critique_points.point_007: Limited generalizability demonstrated beyond two domains with well-defined locality. No experiments or discussion on applicability to other domains with less structured or noisy locality
critique_points.point_008: No error analysis or study of failure cases where locality adjustments may harm performance. The paper does not analyze instances where locality fails to improve or degrades language modeling
